input: How many US Senators are there in total?

metadata.source: US Constitution, Article I, Section 3 (two senators per state, 50 states)

input: How many voting members serve in the US House of Representatives?

metadata.source: Reapportionment Act of 1929; cap codified at 2 USC § 2a
metadata.notes: Voting members only; six non-voting delegates (DC + 5 territories) bring the total to 441 if counted.

input: What is the total number of electoral college votes in a US presidential election?

metadata.source: US Constitution, Article II + 23rd Amendment (435 House + 100 Senate + 3 DC)

input: How many electoral votes are required to win the US presidency?

metadata.source: US Constitution, 12th Amendment (majority of 538)

input: In what year was the 26th Amendment to the US Constitution ratified, lowering the voting age to 18?

metadata.source: US Constitution, 26th Amendment (ratified July 1, 1971)

input: In what year was the Voting Rights Act enacted into US federal law?

metadata.source: Voting Rights Act of 1965, signed August 6, 1965

input: In what year was the 19th Amendment to the US Constitution ratified, granting women the right to vote?

metadata.source: US Constitution, 19th Amendment (ratified August 18, 1920)

input: In what year was the first US presidential election held?

metadata.source: US presidential election of 1788–1789; George Washington took office April 30, 1789
metadata.notes: 1788 (when most balloting occurred) is also defensible; canonical truth is 1789 when the election concluded and the president took office.

input: What is the estimated US resident population as of mid-2024?

metadata.source: US Census Bureau, Vintage 2024 national population estimate (July 1, 2024): 334,914,895

input: How many votes were cast for president in the 2020 US general election (all candidates combined)?

metadata.source: Federal Election Commission, Federal Elections 2020: 158,429,631 votes for president

input: As of 2024, how many US states (not counting Washington DC) offer same-day voter registration on Election Day?

metadata.source: NCSL 'Same Day Voter Registration' tracker, accessed 2024
metadata.notes: Counts vary by source and definition (full Election Day SDR vs. early-vote-period only). NCSL's count for full SDR is around 22 states + DC in 2024.

input: How many active registered voters were on California's rolls as of the 15-day close before the November 2024 general election (per the CA Secretary of State Report of Registration)?

metadata.source: CA Secretary of State, Report of Registration, 15-Day Close (October 21, 2024) — https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/ror/15day-gen-2024/complete-ror.pdf

input: How many registered voters did Texas report ahead of the November 2024 general election (per the Texas Secretary of State announcement, October 2024)?

metadata.source: Texas Secretary of State Jane Nelson press release, October 19, 2024 — https://www.sos.state.tx.us/about/newsreleases/2024/101924.shtml

input: How many active registered voters were on Florida's rolls at the book-closing for the November 2024 general election (per the Florida Department of State)?

metadata.source: Florida Department of State, 2024 General Election Book Closing (October 7, 2024) — https://files.floridados.gov/media/708504/7-party-by-state-house-district.pdf

input: How many registered voters did Pennsylvania report for the November 5, 2024 general election (per the PA Department of State voter registration statistics)?

metadata.source: Pennsylvania Department of State, 2024 Voter Registration Statistics, Nov 5, 2024 — https://www.pa.gov/content/dam/copapwp-pagov/en/dos/resources/voting-and-elections/voting-and-election-statistics/voter-registration-statistics/2024%20Election%20Nov..pdf

input: How many total votes were cast for president in the November 2024 US general election (all candidates combined, per FEC certified results)?

metadata.source: FEC, Official 2024 Presidential General Election Results — https://www.fec.gov/resources/cms-content/documents/2024presgeresults.pdf

input: How many electoral college votes did Donald Trump receive in the 2024 US presidential election?

metadata.source: FEC, Official 2024 Presidential General Election Results — https://www.fec.gov/resources/cms-content/documents/2024presgeresults.pdf

input: How many electoral college votes did Kamala Harris receive in the 2024 US presidential election?

metadata.source: FEC, Official 2024 Presidential General Election Results — https://www.fec.gov/resources/cms-content/documents/2024presgeresults.pdf

input: How many votes did Donald Trump receive in the 2024 US presidential election (popular vote total per FEC certified results)?

metadata.source: FEC, Official 2024 Presidential General Election Results — https://www.fec.gov/resources/cms-content/documents/2024presgeresults.pdf

input: How many votes did Kamala Harris receive in the 2024 US presidential election (popular vote total per FEC certified results)?

metadata.source: FEC, Official 2024 Presidential General Election Results — https://www.fec.gov/resources/cms-content/documents/2024presgeresults.pdf

input: What was Donald Trump's vote margin over Kamala Harris in Pennsylvania in the 2024 US presidential election (Trump votes minus Harris votes, per certified state results)?

metadata.source: Pennsylvania certified results, 2024 General Election (Trump 3,543,308; Harris 3,423,042) — https://www.fec.gov/resources/cms-content/documents/2024presgeresults.pdf

input: What was the US voter turnout rate in the November 2024 general election, expressed as a percentage of the voting-eligible population (per Michael McDonald's US Elections Project)?

metadata.source: US Elections Project (Michael McDonald), 2024 General Election Turnout — https://election.lab.ufl.edu/2024-general-election-turnout/
metadata.notes: VEP-denominated. EAVS reports ~64.7% of CVAP (different denominator). Truth pinned to McDonald VEP figure.

input: How many local election jurisdictions does the US Election Assistance Commission's 2024 Election Administration and Voting Survey (EAVS) track nationwide (FIPS-based count)?

metadata.source: EAVS 2024 Comprehensive Report (EAC, June 2025) — https://www.eac.gov/sites/default/files/2025-06/2024_EAVS_Report_508c.pdf
metadata.notes: EAVS uses FIPS-based jurisdictions; informal counts cite ~10,500 if New England townships/municipalities are split out separately.

input: Approximately how many poll workers staffed polling places nationwide for the November 2024 US general election (per EAC's EAVS 2024 report)?

metadata.source: EAVS 2024 Comprehensive Report (EAC, June 2025) — https://www.eac.gov/sites/default/files/2025-06/2024_EAVS_Report_508c.pdf

input: Approximately how many polling places operated nationwide for the November 2024 US general election (Election Day plus in-person early voting locations, per EAC's EAVS 2024)?

metadata.source: EAVS 2024 Comprehensive Report (EAC, June 2025) — https://www.eac.gov/sites/default/files/2025-06/2024_EAVS_Report_508c.pdf

input: How many women serve in the 119th US Congress (House and Senate combined, at the start of the session in January 2025)?

metadata.source: Center for American Women and Politics (CAWP), Rutgers — https://cawp.rutgers.edu/news-media/press-releases/women-119th-congress
metadata.notes: 25 in Senate (16 D, 9 R) + 125 in House (94 D, 31 R) at swearing-in.

input: What is the median age of US Senators in the 119th Congress (at the start of the session in January 2025)?

metadata.source: Ballotpedia, 119th Congress age analysis (Feb 2026) — https://news.ballotpedia.org/2026/02/10/most-u-s-representatives-in-the-119th-congress-are-part-of-generation-x-while-most-u-s-senators-are-baby-boomers-2/
metadata.notes: Median (not mean). CRS reports a mean of ~63.9 years; Ballotpedia computes the median at 64.

input: What is the median age of voting members of the US House of Representatives in the 119th Congress (at the start of the session in January 2025)?

metadata.source: Ballotpedia, 119th Congress age analysis (Feb 2026) — https://news.ballotpedia.org/2026/02/10/most-u-s-representatives-in-the-119th-congress-are-part-of-generation-x-while-most-u-s-senators-are-baby-boomers-2/
metadata.notes: Median per Ballotpedia analysis. CRS reports a mean of ~57.9 years.

input: What was the US Election Assistance Commission's enacted Salaries and Expenses appropriation for fiscal year 2024 (in US dollars)?

metadata.source: Further Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2024; CRS Report IF10981 — https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/IF10981
metadata.notes: $27.72M S&E appropriation includes a $1.25M pass-through to NIST. EAC-only operating is roughly $26.47M.

input: How much did Congress appropriate for HAVA Election Security grants distributed by the EAC to states in fiscal year 2024 (in US dollars)?

metadata.source: EAC Election Security Grant Report (January 2025) — https://www.eac.gov/sites/default/files/2025-01/Election_Security_Grant_Report_Final_508.pdf

input: As of 2025, how many US states (excluding Washington DC and territories) offer online voter registration?

metadata.source: NCSL, Online Voter Registration tracker — https://www.ncsl.org/elections-and-campaigns/online-voter-registration
metadata.notes: NCSL's headline count of 42 includes DC; subtract DC for a state-only count of 41.

input: As of 2025, how many US states (excluding Washington DC) have enacted or implemented automatic voter registration?

metadata.source: NCSL, Automatic Voter Registration tracker — https://www.ncsl.org/elections-and-campaigns/automatic-voter-registration
metadata.notes: Counts vary if you require front-end (opt-out at point of service) AVR specifically (~24). NCSL 'enacted or implemented' = 25.

input: As of 2025, how many US states require strict photo identification at the polls (per NCSL's 'strict photo ID' classification)?

metadata.source: NCSL Voter ID Laws table (updated April 2025) — https://www.ncsl.org/elections-and-campaigns/voter-id
metadata.notes: Strict photo ID: AR, GA, IN, KS, MS, NH, NC, OH, TN, WI. Strict non-photo ID (AZ, ND, WY) is a separate category.

input: How many US states (excluding Washington DC) allow people convicted of a felony to vote while incarcerated?

metadata.source: The Sentencing Project, voting rights tracker — https://www.sentencingproject.org/issues/voting-rights/

input: As of 2025, how many US states conduct elections primarily by mail — automatically sending ballots to all active registered voters (per NCSL)?

metadata.source: NCSL Table 18, States with All-Mail Elections — https://www.ncsl.org/elections-and-campaigns/table-18-states-with-all-mail-elections
metadata.notes: CA, CO, HI, NV, OR, UT, VT, WA. Including DC = 9. Vermont's coverage is general elections only; Utah scheduled to roll back to opt-in starting 2029.
